interactions.summary: Jiang finishes the collapse argument: if people are infinite resources, terror replaces enslavement; Mongol contempt for Chinese and other local cultures makes governance impossible; efforts to adopt Chinese bureaucracy clash with Mongol nobility and help collapse the empire.

A student named Doug says he has no question and is scared to comment because the class is on YouTube; Jiang invites comments and questions, and the transcript then contains brief non-substantive backchannel fragments.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang and student/audience; some backchannel diarization is noisy.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_00 appears to be a student or audience member, probably Doug, replying to Jiang's invitation.
Segments contain repeated 'Yup' backchannels and are not substantive questions.

claims.claim: If people are treated as an infinite resource, massacre for terror becomes more strategically useful than preserving conquered people as slaves.
Mongol contempt for local culture, especially in Yuan China, made rule unstable and contributed to Chinese rebellion.
Attempts by Mongol leadership to adopt Chinese-style bureaucracy conflicted with Mongol nobility's desire to preserve Mongolian culture, producing internal tension that led to collapse.

signature_moments.moment: Infinite people means you do not want slaves; you want bodies converted into terror.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you don't want them as slaves. You want to kill as many people as possible in order to inflict terror
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the brutal moral consequence of the infinite-resource idea.

signature_moments.moment: The Mongol emperor needs assimilation, but the nobility needs Mongol difference.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the nobility and the emperor couldn't agree
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It compresses collapse into an institutional contradiction between rule and identity.

signature_moments.moment: A student declines to comment because the class is on YouTube.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I'm kind of scared. This is on YouTube
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The aside reveals the room's awareness that frank historical interpretation is being publicly recorded.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's example of Mongol contempt in China: Mongols at the top, Chinese at the bottom, foreigners in between.
The administrative system Mongol leaders tried to adopt to govern empire, clashing with the nobility's resistance to assimilation.

chronology_notes.note: This packet contains the lecture's explicit collapse explanation for Yuan/Mongol rule and should anchor any later corpus-impact note on conquest logic becoming governance failure.

uncertainty_notes.note: Repeated 'Yup' spans are diarization/ASR noise or low-content backchannels and should not be promoted as substantive questions.
